claim: Open Claw should run on a dedicated machine separate from your daily driver for security and 24/7 availability of agents
actionable: Set up Open Claw on a dedicated Mac Mini or VPS rather than running it on your personal laptop

claim: Use API tokens through OpenRouter for Open Claw rather than relying on Claude Max subscription to avoid account suspension
actionable: Configure Open Claw agents to use OpenRouter API keys instead of personal Claude Max subscriptions

claim: Use different AI models for different agent roles: Opus for reasoning-heavy roles, Sonnet for marketing/general tasks
actionable: Assign Opus models to developer/admin agents and Sonnet models to marketing/assistant agents to optimize cost vs capability

claim: Set up agents with least-privilege access: separate email, limited GitHub access, isolated Dropbox accounts instead of granting full personal access
actionable: Create dedicated service accounts for agents with only necessary permissions to company tools and resources

claim: Building a custom dashboard for agent management helps track token costs and assign specific tasks to specific agents
actionable: Create a Rails or similar app that displays queued tasks, token usage metrics, and allows assigning work to named agents

claim: Multi-agent systems with shared workspace allow better coordination than separate isolated agents
actionable: Configure all agents to access a shared memory space and a central brain folder for coordinated decision-making
